name: passagemath-sws2rst

metadata.name: passagemath-sws2rst
metadata.license: GNU General Public License (GPL) v3 or later

documentation:
sage_sws2rst: Translate legacy Sage worksheet files (.sws) to reStructuredText (.rst) files
===========================================================================================

Description
-----------

Provides a script `sage-sws2rst`, which translates a Sage worksheet file (.sws) into a reStructuredText (.rst) file.

Sage worksheet files (.sws) are a file format that was used by the now-obsolete Sage notebook (https://github.com/sagemath/sagenb), superseded by the Jupyter notebook.  SageNB was dropped in the course of the transition of SageMath to Python 3.

This package was extracted from the SageNB sources in https://github.com/sagemath/sage/issues/28838 to provide a way to convert pedagogical material written available in Sage worksheet format.

modules.name: sage_sws2rst.results2rst
documentation:
Convert output from code cells in the notebook into ReStructuredText

This is called by sws2rst

- Pablo Angulo Ardoy (2011-02-25): initial version
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.results2rst.results2rst
documentation:
Converts the result of evaluation of notebook cells into rst compatible with Sage documentation. Several common patterns are identified, and treated accordingly. Some patterns are dropped, while others are not recognized. Currently, latex and images are recognized and converted. INPUT: - `text` -- string -- a chunk of HTML text - `images_dir` -- string -- folder where images are stored OUTPUT: - string -- rst text EXAMPLES: ``` >>> from sage_sws2rst.results2rst import results2rst >>> s="" >>> results2rst(s,'') '\n.. image:: sage0.png\n :align: center\n' >>> results2rst("4",'') ' 4 ' >>> s=r'
\newcommand{\Bold}[1]{\mathbf{#1}}\frac{3}{2}
' >>> results2rst(s,'') '\n.. MATH::\n\n \\frac{3}{2}\n' ```

classes.name: sage_sws2rst.results2rst.States
documentation: We parse lines one by one but keep track of current scope
similarly to worksheet2rst.py
Results are split into different types. Some are discarded

classes.name: sage_sws2rst.results2rst.ResultsParser
documentation: Auxiliary class for results2rst

modules.name: sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst
documentation:
Convert worksheet.html files into ReStructuredText documents

This is called by 'sage -sws2rst'. Can also be used as a commandline script
(if BeautifulSoup is installed):

`python worksheet2rst.py worksheet.html`

or

`cat worksheet.html | python worksheet2rst.py`

AUTHOR:

- Pablo Angulo Ardoy (2011-02-25): initial version

The content of worksheet.html is split into comments, code, and output
(the result of evaluating the code), as follows:

comments
{{{id=..|
code
///
results
}}}

Each kind of text is dealt with separately.
!/usr/bin/python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst.code_parser
documentation:
> Arguments:
>
> INPUT:
>
> - `s`:sage code, may or may not start with "sage:"
>
> OUTPUT:
>
> - string -- rst text
>
> EXAMPLES (not used for unit test, see
> <http://groups.google.com/group/sage-devel/browse_thread/thread/d82cb049ac102f3a>)
>
> \: from sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst import code_parser
> \: s="a=2"
> \: code_parser(s)
> ':
>
> sage: a=2'
> \: s="def f(n):
> return n+1

"

: \: code_parser(s)
  ':

  sage: def f(n):
  ....: return n+1'
  \: s="sage: def f(n):

sage: return n+1
"

> \: code_parser(s)
> ':
>
> sage: def f(n):
> ....: return n+1'

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst.worksheet2rst
documentation:
Parses a string, tipically the content of the file : worksheet.html inside a sws file, and converts it into rst compatible with Sage documentation. INPUT: - `s` -- string -- text, tipically the content of : worksheet.html - `images_dir` -- string -- folder where images are stored OUTPUT: - string -- rst text EXAMPLES (not used for unit test, see ) \: from sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst import worksheet2rst \: worksheet2rst('\

some text\

{{{id=1| print 2+2 /// 4 }}}') > u'.. -*- coding: utf-8 -*- # Please write a title for this worksheet! some text ``` sage: print 2+2 4 ``` % end of output ' : \: s = '{{{id=2| show(f) /// \\
sqrt\{x}\
\ }}} ' > \: worksheet2rst(s) > u'.. -*- coding: utf-8 -*- # Please write a title for this worksheet! ``` sage: show(f) ``` ```{MATH} \sqrt{x} ``` % end of output '

classes.name: sage_sws2rst.worksheet2rst.States
documentation: We parse lines one by one but keep track of current scope
comments
code
results
RESULT_TO_BE_DROPPED corresponds to a results section whose
code was empty, and will be discarded, whether it's empty or not

modules.name: sage_sws2rst.comments2rst
documentation:
Convert html from text cells in the notebook into ReStructuredText

This is called by sws2rst

- Pablo Angulo Ardoy (2011-02-25): initial version
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.comments2rst.preprocess_display_latex
documentation:
replace \$\$some display latex\$\$ with \some display latex\ before the soup is built. Deals with the situation where \

\

tags are mixed with \$\$, like \$\$\

display_latex\$\$\

, unless the mess is huge EXAMPLES: ``` >>> from sage_sws2rst.comments2rst import preprocess_display_latex >>> s="$$a=2$$" >>> preprocess_display_latex(s) 'a=2' >>> s="

$$a=2$$

" >>> preprocess_display_latex(s) '

a=2

' >>> s="

$$a=2

$$" >>> preprocess_display_latex(s) '

a=2

' >>> s="$$

a=2

$$" >>> preprocess_display_latex(s) 'a=2' ```

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.comments2rst.replace_courier
documentation:
Lacking a better option, I use courier font to mark <code>
within tinyMCE. And I want to turn that into real code tags.

Most users won't be needing this(?), so this code is not called anywhere
but kept for reference

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.comments2rst.replace_latex
documentation:
Replaces inline latex by $code$ and escapes some rst special chars like +, -, * and | outside of inline latex does not escape chars inside display or pre tags EXAMPLES: ``` >>> from sage_sws2rst.comments2rst import replace_latex >>> from bs4 import BeautifulSoup >>> soup = r"

Some latex: $e^\pi i=-1$

" >>> s = BeautifulSoup(soup, features='html.parser') >>> replace_latex(s) >>> s

Some latex: :math:`e^\pi i=-1`

``` ``` >>> soup = "

2+2 | 1+3

" >>> s = BeautifulSoup(soup, features='html.parser') >>> replace_latex(s) >>> s

2\+2 \| 1\+3

```

functions.name: sage_sws2rst.comments2rst.html2rst
documentation:
Convert html, typically generated by tinyMCE, into rst compatible with Sage documentation. The main job is done by BeautifulSoup, which is much more robust than conventional parsers like HTMLParser, but also several details specific of this context are taken into account, so this code differs from generic approaches like those found on the web. INPUT: - `text` -- string -- a chunk of HTML text - `images_dir` -- string -- folder where images are stored OUTPUT: - string -- rst text EXAMPLES: ``` >>> from sage_sws2rst.comments2rst import html2rst >>> text = r'

Some text with math: $e^{\pi i}=-1$

' >>> html2rst(text, '') 'Some text with *math* : :math:`e^{\\pi i}=-1`\n\n' ``` ``` >>> text = '

Text with incorrect

nesting.' >>> html2rst(text, '') 'Text with *incorrect* \n\n nesting\n.' ``` ``` >>> text = '
Preformatted: \n    a+2\n

Not preformatted: \n a+2\n

' >>> html2rst(text, '') '::\n\n Preformatted: \n a+2\n \n Not preformatted: a\\+2\n\n' ``` ``` >>> text = 'áñ ñá' >>> html2rst(text, '') '\xe1\xf1 \xf1\xe1' ``` ``` >>> text = r'

some text

$$

3.183098861 \cdot 10^{-1}

$$

' >>> html2rst(text, '') 'some text\n\n.. MATH::\n\n 3.183098861 \\cdot 10^{-1}\n\n.. end of math\n\n' ``` When the content is empty: ``` >>> html2rst(" ", '') '\n ' >>> html2rst(" ", '') '\n ' >>> html2rst(" ", '') '\n ' >>> html2rst(" ", '') '\n ' ``` Spaces are added around *italic* or **bold** text (otherwise, it may be invalid ReStructuredText syntax): ``` >>> text = '

Exercice.Let x be ...

' >>> html2rst(text, '') ' **Exercice.** Let x be ...\n\n' >>> text = '

Exercice.Let x be ...

' >>> html2rst(text, '') ' *Exercice.* Let x be ...\n\n' ``` Below is an example showing the translation from html to rst is not always perfect. Here the strong emphasis is on more than one line and is not properly translated: ``` >>> text='

You will find a while loop helpful here. Below is a simple example:

x = 0
while x < 7:
    x = x + 2
    print x

' >>> html2rst(text, '') 'You will find a *while loop* helpful here. Below is a simple example:\n\n **x = 0 \n\n while x < 7: \n\n x = x \\+ 2 \n\n print x** \n\n' ```

builds the rst text from the Soup Tree
